About.Description: Better and simpler magnification tool for Windows.
About.Help: To activate hold the 'Enable' key and either scroll the mouse wheel or use +/- on your keyboard.
About.HowToUseDetailed:
Hold the Enable key and scroll the mouse wheel, or use +/- to zoom.
Use the invert hotkey to toggle inverted colors.
QuickZoom is a lightweight, open-source alternative to Windows Magnifier for Windows 10/11 x64.
Dialog.PromptKeyDefaultDescription: Press a single key (letters, F-keys, Ctrl/Alt/Shift). Win key may be reserved by Windows.
Dialog.PromptKeyDefaultTitle: Press the key you want to hold while scrolling
Error.KeyboardHookFailed: Failed to set low-level keyboard hook.
Error.MagnifierInit: QuickZoom could not initialize monitor magnification windows on this system. Magnification was turned off to avoid black overlays.
Error.MouseHookFailed: Failed to set low-level mouse hook.
Settings.AboutLocationsHelp: Open the install or config folder.
Settings.AutoDisableAt100Help: Turns magnification off when you return to 100%.
Settings.AutoSwitchMonitorHelp: Follows the monitor your cursor moves onto.
Settings.CenterCursorHelp: Keeps the focus point more centered while zooming.
Settings.CreditsHint: A small note about who made QuickZoom and the support behind it.
Settings.DisplayHint: Use the tray quick panel whenever you want to change which displays are magnified right now.
Settings.DisplayCustomMonitorHelp: Include this monitor in the magnified view when custom selection is active.
Settings.DisplaySelectionModeHelp: Choose whether QuickZoom magnifies all displays, the monitor under your cursor, or a custom monitor selection.
Settings.DisplaySectionHint: Decide how QuickZoom chooses and tracks the active monitor while zooming.
Settings.EnableKeyDialogBody: Press the key you want to hold while scrolling to magnify.
Settings.EnableKeyHelp: Primary QuickZoom key used with mouse wheel zoom and +/- keyboard zoom.
Settings.GeneralSectionHint: Smooth movement and predictable zoom behavior should feel effortless.
Settings.InputSectionHint: Choose the shortcut combinations that feel most natural for your workflow.
Settings.ShortcutModeHelp: Choose whether QuickZoom accepts both mouse and keyboard shortcuts, keyboard only, or mouse only.
Settings.InvertActivationKeyHelp: Used with the primary key. Middle mouse click stays available as the default mouse shortcut.
Settings.FollowCursorHotkeyHelp: Used with the primary key to toggle Follow Cursor. No mouse shortcut is used for this action.
Settings.FollowCursorHotkeyDialogBody: Press the key you want to use with the primary QuickZoom key to toggle Follow Cursor.
Settings.InvertHotkeyHelp: Choose the shortcut that toggles inverted colors.
Settings.InvertKeyDialogBody: Press the key you want to use to toggle invert colors.
Settings.LanguageHelp: Changes the tray, settings window, and dialogs.
Settings.MaxZoomHelp: Sets the highest zoom level QuickZoom can reach.
Settings.MoreLanguages: More languages can be added later without changing the layout.
Settings.RefreshRateHelp: Higher values can feel smoother but use more resources.
Settings.SmoothZoomHelp: Blends between zoom levels for softer transitions.
Settings.Subtitle: QuickZoom options.
Settings.ThemeModeHelp: Auto follows Windows, while Light and Dark stay fixed.
Settings.Title: QuickZoom Settings
Settings.WiggleSpotlightHelp: Quickly wiggle the mouse to highlight the cursor.
Settings.ZoomStepHelp: Sets how much each zoom step changes.
Startup.AdminRequiredBody: QuickZoom needs one Administrator approval to create the elevated startup task.
Startup.ErrorMissingExePath: QuickZoom could not determine its current executable path.
Startup.ErrorMissingUser: QuickZoom could not determine the current Windows user.
Startup.ErrorPowerShellFailed: PowerShell failed while registering the elevated startup task.
Startup.ErrorPowerShellLaunch: QuickZoom could not start PowerShell to register the startup task.
Startup.ErrorPowerShellTimeout: PowerShell timed out while registering the elevated startup task.
Startup.ErrorUnexpected: QuickZoom encountered an unexpected error while registering the startup task.
Startup.InstallPromptInstallBody:
QuickZoom will copy itself into a permanent folder in your user profile and create the elevated startup service there.

That keeps startup working even if this EXE is moved or deleted.
Startup.InstallPromptInstallHeading: Install QuickZoom to a permanent startup location?
Startup.InstallPromptUpdateBody:
QuickZoom will copy this build into its permanent folder and update the startup service to use it.

That keeps startup working even if this downloaded EXE is moved or deleted.
Startup.InstallPromptUpdateHeading: Update the installed QuickZoom startup copy?
Startup.NotElevatedAfterFailedSetupBody:
QuickZoom could not complete elevated startup setup.

It will continue in normal user mode, but zoom hotkeys may not work while an Administrator app is focused.
Startup.NotElevatedBody:
QuickZoom will continue in normal user mode.

Zoom hotkeys may not work while an Administrator app is focused until you set up elevated startup.
Startup.SetupCopyFailedBody:
QuickZoom could not copy itself into its permanent startup location.

Please try again.
Startup.SetupSuccessBody: QuickZoom was installed to a permanent startup location and will now be able to launch elevated automatically at sign-in without prompting each boot.
Startup.TempLocationBody:
QuickZoom will continue in normal user mode from this EXE.

If you want startup support that keeps working after the file is moved or deleted, set up the permanent startup copy.
Startup.TempLocationHeading: QuickZoom is running from a temporary location
Tray.DisplayAllHint: Keep all connected monitors in the same zoomed view.
Tray.DisplayCursorHint: Magnify whichever monitor the cursor is currently on.
Tray.DisplaySummaryCursor: QuickZoom follows where the cursor is present.
Tray.ResetCursorHint: Refresh the Windows cursor feel if it seems off.
